description:
You are the on–call operations engineer and need to triage a payment-platform incident that happened on 2023-10-15 between 14:00 UTC and 16:00 UTC.  
The raw logs for that day have already been collected on the local machine:

1. /home/user/logs/sys01-2023-10-15.log (plain text)  
2. /home/user/logs/sys02-2023-10-15.log (plain text)  
3. /home/user/logs/archive/sys03-2023-10-15.log.gz (gzip archive)

Task goal  
Create a **single, machine-readable CSV summary** of every distinct error code that occurred during the incident window (14:00 UTC through 15:59:59 UTC).  
Store your summary at  

/home/user/incident_reports/2023-10-15_error_summary.csv  

and make sure the directory /home/user/incident_reports/ exists (create it if necessary).

CSV specification (exactly this structure, no extra spaces):
• The file must be UTF-8 encoded text.  
• The first (header) row must be:  
  error_code,total_occurrences,first_seen,last_seen,affected_services  
• Subsequent rows:  
  – Sorted lexicographically by error_code (ascending).  
  – One row per distinct error_code found in the target interval.  
  – Columns are comma-separated, in this order:  
    1. error_code: literal error identifier (e.g. E1024)  
    2. total_occurrences: integer count in the interval  
    3. first_seen: earliest timestamp (ISO-8601 as it appears in the log line) within the interval for that code  
    4. last_seen: latest timestamp within the interval for that code  
    5. affected_services: unique set of service names that emitted that code, joined by “;” (semicolon) and sorted alphabetically

• Do not include any records whose timestamps are **before 14:00 UTC or on/after 16:00 UTC**.

Examples of valid data rows (illustrative only):  
E1234,7,2023-10-15T14:02:11Z,2023-10-15T15:58:44Z,billing;checkout

Additional requirements
A. You must extract data from **all three files**, handling the compressed one directly or by uncompressing it.  
B. Filtering, aggregation and sorting can be done using any standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities you deem appropriate.  
C. When finished, list the relative path of every command you used (one per line) in a human-readable run log stored as  
   /home/user/incident_reports/2023-10-15_run.log  
   (The automated checker will ignore this file; it is only for a peer engineer’s reference.)

Success criteria checked by the automated tests
1. The directory /home/user/incident_reports/ exists.  
2. The file /home/user/incident_reports/2023-10-15_error_summary.csv exists.  
3. The CSV is exactly conformant to the specification above, including header, row order, field separators and values.

Hint: if you process large files in parallel, remember to merge intermediate outputs before the final sort so the counts are correct.
truth:

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

2023-10-15T15:10:00Z [service:checkout] ERROR E2048 Inventory mismatch for product 654
2023-10-15T15:20:17Z [service:search]  ERROR E3001 Search backend timeout

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

2. /home/user/logs/sys02-2023-10-15.log  
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

2023-10-15T14:50:22Z [service:user]    ERROR E5000 Unable to fetch profile
2023-10-15T15:05:36Z [service:user]    ERROR E5000 Unable to fetch profile
2023-10-15T15:22:01Z [service:search]  ERROR E3001 Search backend timeout
2023-10-15T15:45:59Z [service:checkout] ERROR E2048 Inventory mismatch for product 321
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

3. /home/user/logs/archive/sys03-2023-10-15.log.gz  (gzip, single-member)  
Uncompressed contents:  
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2023-10-15T14:30:00Z [service:billing] ERROR E1024 Card declined for user 999
2023-10-15T15:12:12Z [service:search]  ERROR E3001 Search backend timeout
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Expected final artefact created by the agent:

/home/user/incident_reports/2023-10-15_error_summary.csv  
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
error_code,total_occurrences,first_seen,last_seen,affected_services
E1024,4,2023-10-15T14:05:12Z,2023-10-15T14:45:00Z,billing
E2048,3,2023-10-15T14:15:50Z,2023-10-15T15:45:59Z,checkout
E3001,3,2023-10-15T15:12:12Z,2023-10-15T15:22:01Z,search
E5000,2,2023-10-15T14:50:22Z,2023-10-15T15:05:36Z,user
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

(The run-log file /home/user/incident_reports/2023-10-15_run.log may contain anything; it is *not* part of automated grading.)

Directory permissions: the entire /home/user tree is owned by the unprivileged user “user”; no special privilege escalation is required.
